Significant Findings

ALL_PRIMARY_OR
All Primary Pathway Genes EFS

ALL_PRIMARY_OR pathway alteration was associated with significantly worse event-free survival (median EFS: 9.9 vs. NR months; log-rank p &lt;0.001; HR = 4.21 [2.90–6.12]).

4. OR-Classifier Analysis

OR-classifiers aggregate all patients with any alteration within the pathway. A significant OR-classifier result indicates pathway-level prognostic impact even when individual genes are too rare to analyze independently.

5. Expression Analysis

mRNA z-scores from cBioPortal were used to stratify patients into HIGH (z>threshold) and LOW (z≤threshold) expression groups. Default threshold z=1.0; IGF2 uses z=1.5. Analyses performed only for PDMR-focus genes with sufficient sample sizes.
No expression analyses reached statistical significance (p<0.05). Notable trend: MDM4 high expression showed a borderline EFS association (p=0.059, HR=1.40 [0.94–2.07]).

7. Summary of Findings

Significant Associations (6 results)

Across 1 Wilms tumor cohort from cBioPortal (N = 652, Pediatric Wilms' Tumor TARGET 2018), Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazards analysis identified the following statistically significant survival associations:

OR-Classifier Findings

  • ALL_PRIMARY_OR (TP53, MYCN, SIX1, SIX2, DROSHA, DGCR8, CTNNB1, AMER1, WT1, MLLT1, NIPBL): EFS median 9.9 vs. NR months; HR = 4.21 [2.90–6.12], p &lt;0.001
  • MYCN_SIX_OR (MYCN, SIX1/SIX2): EFS median 10.5 vs. NR months; HR = 3.78 [2.31–6.19], p &lt;0.001
  • TP53_MDM4_MDM2_OR (TP53, MDM4, MDM2): OS median NR vs. NR months; HR = 3.55 [1.85–6.83], p &lt;0.001
  • ALL_PRIMARY_OR (TP53, MYCN, SIX1, SIX2, DROSHA, DGCR8, CTNNB1, AMER1, WT1, MLLT1, NIPBL): OS median NR vs. NR months; HR = 3.38 [2.06–5.54], p &lt;0.001
  • MYCN_SIX_OR (MYCN, SIX1/SIX2): OS median NR vs. NR months; HR = 3.27 [1.70–6.31], p &lt;0.001
  • TP53_MDM4_MDM2_OR (TP53, MDM4, MDM2): EFS median 14.2 vs. NR months; HR = 2.34 [1.33–4.11], p 0.003

Individual Gene Findings

  • No individual genes reached significance at the n≥20 threshold.

Non-Significant Genes

The following genes were analyzed but did not reach statistical significance (p≥0.05) or were below the minimum-events gate: None below threshold..

Note: Some genes were excluded from KM analysis due to insufficient events (n_altered < 20 required by the pre-specified gate).

Expression Analysis Summary

mRNA z-score–based survival analysis did not identify statistically significant associations after multiple testing consideration. Noteworthy trends:

  • MDM4 (EFS): p = 0.059, HR = 1.40 [0.94–2.07]
  • MDM2 (OS): p = 0.077, HR = 0.55 [0.28–1.07]
  • SIX2 (OS): p = 0.123, HR = 1.67 [0.88–3.17]
  • MYCN (OS): p = 0.136, HR = 1.57 [0.86–2.85]
  • MLLT1 (OS): p = 0.148, HR = 1.61 [0.82–3.18]
  • MLLT1 (EFS): p = 0.199, HR = 1.35 [0.80–2.29]
  • MYCN (EFS): p = 0.223, HR = 1.32 [0.83–2.11]
  • MDM4 (OS): p = 0.401, HR = 1.26 [0.73–2.15]
  • CTNNB1 (OS): p = 0.418, HR = 0.74 [0.35–1.58]
  • CTNNB1 (EFS): p = 0.485, HR = 0.85 [0.50–1.43]
  • MDM2 (EFS): p = 0.554, HR = 1.08 [0.70–1.65]
  • SIX2 (EFS): p = 0.603, HR = 1.21 [0.72–2.05]

MDM4 high expression showed a borderline EFS association (p≈0.059) that warrants follow-up in a larger expression cohort.

Recommended Next Steps

  • Validate TP53/MDM4/MDM2 pathway findings in an independent cohort (e.g., TARGET GDC or SIOP datasets)
  • Investigate MDM4 expression trend (p=0.059) with larger sample sizes or alternative z-score thresholds
  • Perform multivariable analysis controlling for stage and histology alongside molecular markers
  • Examine interaction between OR-classifier status and treatment protocol (NWTS-4 vs. NTWS-4)
  • Consider functional validation of MYCN/SIX1-SIX2 axis findings in Wilms tumor preclinical models
  • Explore IGF2 expression stratification with a CNA-aware approach

8. Methods

Data source: Clinical and genomic data were retrieved from cBioPortal (cbioportal.org) for the Pediatric Wilms' Tumor (TARGET, 2018) study (study ID: wt_target_2018_pub; N=652 patients).

Genomic alteration classification: Mutations and copy-number alterations were combined into a binary "altered/wildtype" flag per gene. OR-classifiers were constructed by flagging any patient with at least one alteration in the specified gene set.

Minimum events gate: Individual gene analyses required ≥20 patients in the altered group to ensure statistical power. Genes below this threshold were excluded from KM and Cox analyses.

Survival analysis: Kaplan-Meier curves were estimated using the lifelines library (v0.27+). Log-rank tests were used for univariate comparison. Cox proportional hazards models were fit using age as a covariate (ALTERED + AGE). Primary endpoint was event-free survival (EFS); overall survival (OS) was a secondary endpoint.

Expression analysis: mRNA z-scores (relative to diploid samples) were obtained from cBioPortal. Patients were stratified into HIGH (z>1.0; z>1.5 for IGF2) and LOW (z≤threshold) groups. Log-rank and Cox analyses were performed as above.

Report generated: June 24, 2026 using Python (pandas, lifelines, matplotlib). All analyses were conducted using the cBioPortal REST API and publicly available data.
